title: Paramoltkia doerfleri
taxonomy: Paramoltkia doerfleri belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a member of the genus Paramoltkia, which is a small group of plants native to New Guinea. The plant is also known as the 'Blue Chalksticks' due to its unique, upright, blue-gray foliage. Within the plant kingdom, it is classified as a shrub or a subshrub. As a flowering plant, it produces small white or pink flowers that grow in clusters at the tips of its stems.

intro: Paramoltkia doerfleri, also known as the Turkish angelica or Doerfler's angelica, is a plant species native to Turkey and is used in traditional medicine. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Paramoltkia doerfleri include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Paramoltkia doerfleri is commonly used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
